name: HydraFacial
blurb: Deep-clean, exfoliate and flood the skin with hydration in one gentle session.
whatItIs: A medical-grade hydradermabrasion that cleanses, gently exfoliates, extracts congestion and infuses serums for instant radiance.
evidence: A popular, gentle resurfacing-and-hydration facial prized for immediate radiance. Widely offered as standard care; rigorous long-term studies are limited, so think of it as a glow-and-hydration treat rather than a clinically proven fix.

name: Microneedling
blurb: Stimulate your own collagen to refine scars, texture and fine lines.
whatItIs: Fine needles create micro-channels that trigger collagen and elastin renewal; can be paired with PRP for an extra boost.
evidence: Reviewed evidence (2025 systematic review, 21 studies / 723 patients) supports microneedling for wrinkles, texture, photoaging and laxity, and it is well regarded for acne scarring. It is not a treatment for active acne.

name: Chemical Peel Facial
blurb: Lift away dull, uneven surface cells to reveal fresher skin underneath.
whatItIs: A professional acid blend, tailored to your skin, that accelerates exfoliation to brighten tone and smooth texture.
downtime: A few days of light flaking
sessions: Every 3–4 weeks in a short series
evidence: A long-established way to improve tone, texture and superficial pigment; strength is matched to your skin. For deeper pigment or darker skin tones a careful, conservative plan reduces the risk of post-inflammatory marks.

name: Dermaplaning Facial
blurb: Instantly smooth, glow and remove peach fuzz with zero downtime.
whatItIs: A sterile blade gently removes dead surface cells and fine vellus hair, leaving skin luminous and makeup-ready.
evidence: A gentle physical exfoliation that smooths and brightens immediately; one of the few resurfacing options generally considered fine during pregnancy.

name: Acne Facial
blurb: A deep-cleansing, calming facial to clear congestion and support clearer skin.
whatItIs: Targeted cleansing, gentle exfoliation and extractions to decongest pores and soothe active breakouts.
evidence: Supportive, deep-cleansing care. Evidence specifically for facials clearing acne is limited, so it works best alongside a medical acne plan (the proven first-line is a topical retinoid plus benzoyl peroxide) from your provider.

name: Vitamin C Facial
blurb: Antioxidant brightening for a radiant, more even complexion.
whatItIs: A vitamin C–rich facial that brightens, defends against environmental stress and revives dull skin.
evidence: Vitamin C is a well-supported antioxidant for brightening and a more even tone, and pairs naturally with daily sunscreen.

name: Glass Facial with Salmon DNA
blurb: The viral 'glass skin' glow, deep hydration, bounce and luminosity.
whatItIs: A polynucleotide (salmon-DNA/PDRN) infusion facial that hydrates, plumps and supports skin renewal for a dewy finish.
evidence: Polynucleotide (PDRN) treatments are an emerging, well-tolerated option for hydration and skin quality. Current studies are small and low-to-moderate quality, so results are promising but not yet firmly proven.

name: Gua Sha Stone Facial
blurb: A sculpting, circulation-boosting massage for a lifted, de-puffed look.
whatItIs: Traditional stone-massage techniques that encourage circulation and lymphatic flow for an instantly refreshed complexion.
evidence: A relaxing massage that can leave skin looking refreshed and de-puffed. Rigorous clinical evidence is minimal, so we offer it as a wellness and relaxation treatment with mostly immediate, temporary effects.

name: Cupping Facial
blurb: Gentle suction to invigorate circulation, plumpness and a healthy flush.
whatItIs: Soft facial cups lift and stimulate the skin to encourage circulation and a refreshed, plumped appearance.
evidence: A gentle, relaxing facial that stimulates circulation. Clinical evidence is minimal, so we present it as a wellness treatment with mostly short-lived, cosmetic effects.

name: Manual Lymphatic Drainage Facial
blurb: Featherlight massage to de-puff, brighten and calm.
whatItIs: Gentle rhythmic massage that encourages lymphatic flow to reduce puffiness and support skin recovery.
evidence: A soothing, low-risk treatment that can reduce puffiness and support recovery, especially after other procedures. Effects are gentle and temporary.

name: V-Tox + Carboxy Combo
blurb: An oxygenating, glow-boosting combo that wakes up tired, congested skin.
whatItIs: Carboxytherapy delivers CO2 to boost microcirculation and oxygenation, paired with a brightening V-Tox treatment for double the glow.
evidence: Carboxytherapy aims to improve microcirculation and oxygenation. Evidence is limited, so we offer it as an emerging glow and under-eye treatment rather than a proven one.

name: BOTOX®
blurb: Soften frown lines, forehead lines and crow's feet at the source.
whatItIs: A precise neuromodulator that relaxes the muscles behind expression lines for a smoother, refreshed look.
downtime: None, just avoid lying down for 4 hours
evidence: An FDA-approved, gold-standard treatment for softening dynamic expression lines. Per FDA labeling it is avoided with any infection at the injection site or a known botulinum-toxin allergy, and is conventionally deferred during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

name: Juvéderm Fillers
blurb: Restore volume, smooth folds and refine contours, reversibly.
whatItIs: Hyaluronic-acid fillers that replace lost volume, soften deeper folds and sculpt features such as cheeks, lips and tear troughs.
downtime: Possible swelling or bruising for a few days
evidence: FDA-approved hyaluronic-acid fillers that restore volume and contour and are dissolvable if ever needed. Deferred with active injection-site infection and conventionally during pregnancy or breastfeeding.

name: ClearLift Laser Rejuvenation
blurb: A 'lunchtime' laser that rebuilds collagen with no downtime.
whatItIs: A gentle non-ablative laser that heats deeper layers to stimulate collagen and firm skin, without injuring the surface.
evidence: Non-ablative laser rejuvenation gently stimulates collagen for gradual firming and tone, prized for having essentially no downtime. Deeper skin tones are assessed first to protect against pigment changes.

name: IPL Photorejuvenation
blurb: Erase sun spots and diffuse redness for clearer, more even skin.
whatItIs: Intense pulsed light targets brown spots, broken capillaries and overall sun damage to even out tone.
downtime: Spots may darken then flake over a week
evidence: Intense pulsed light is well established for sun-related brown spots and redness. It is not used for melasma, and recently tanned or deeper (Fitzpatrick IV–VI) skin tones need specialist assessment first to avoid pigment changes.

name: Laser Spot Treatment
blurb: Pinpoint stubborn brown or red spots in a quick, focused session.
whatItIs: Targeted laser energy breaks up one to five discrete brown or red spots for a clearer, more even canvas.
downtime: The spot darkens then flakes off in 7–10 days
evidence: A focused, efficient way to clear individual sun spots and discrete pigment. Deeper or melasma-type pigment needs a tailored plan, and darker skin tones are assessed first.

name: Dye-VL for Redness & Rosacea
blurb: Calm facial flushing, rosacea and visible vessels with vascular light.
whatItIs: A vascular light therapy that targets the dilated vessels behind redness, rosacea and facial veins.
downtime: Possible brief redness or darkening of vessels
evidence: Light- and laser-based vascular treatments are a mainstay for facial redness, rosacea and visible vessels (rosacea care is tailored to your individual features), with results that build over a short series.
